what_it_does: A standalone @dsCard preview (group 'Brand') showing the five status colours — success, warning, error, info, pending — each as a soft-fill chip with a coloured status dot and a labelled pill, plus name and hex.
observations: Comment header `@dsCard group="Brand" name="Status colors" subtitle="Warm-shifted success / warning / error / info / pending" viewport="700x220"`.
Five statuses: success #5A8A4A (Approved), warning #E5A547 (Review), error #C24A3C (Rejected), info #4A78B8 (Info), pending #E5C547 (Pending).
Each chip pairs a soft tinted background (e.g. #E6EFDB) with a saturated dot and a darker-toned pill label.
Lead text: 'Status — warm-shifted, desaturated to sit inside the ivory palette. Pending uses gold, not amber.'
Pill labels show domain-flavoured states (Approved/Review/Rejected) tying colours to workflow semantics.
standouts: The deliberate rule 'Pending uses gold, not amber' distinguishes pending (#E5C547) from warning (#E5A547).
Each status carries three coordinated tones: soft bg, dot, and pill text — a small per-status palette.
conventions: Hardcodes all status hexes inline (expected for a colour-definition card) while using tokens for layout chrome.
notes: A swatch card defining the five warm-shifted status colours, each with bg/dot/pill tones and a workflow label.
questions: Are the per-status soft-bg and pill-text shades (e.g. #E6EFDB, #3D6431) themselves tokenised, or derived only here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — metadata names the five warm-shifted statuses and the body shows each with its coordinated tones.
apparent_themes: Warm/desaturated status palette tuned to the ivory surface family
Status as a small per-colour set (bg + dot + label)
intention_signals: Aims to define a status colour system that reads as part of the warm palette rather than generic traffic-light colours, and to separate gold-pending from amber-warning.
novelty: The pending-is-gold-not-amber distinction and the warm-shifted, palette-integrated status colours.
contribution: Contributes the canonical status-colour set (five states, each with bg/dot/pill tones and workflow labels) that a fused system would keep as its status-semantics reference.
summary_for_embedding: A Brand-group design-system preview card showing the five warm-shifted status colours (success, warning, error, info, pending) each with soft background, dot, and pill-label tones and hex values.
intention_for_embedding: To define warm, palette-integrated status colours and their workflow meanings, distinguishing gold pending from amber warning.
